name: FlexRow
summary: Intrinsic auto-flow container that flows and wraps its children by the space actually available to it, realizing the Breakpoint Intent at scope:container. Native-first: the default is CSS @container queries / flex-wrap; ResizeObserver is named as the implementation substrate for environments without container-query support (impl-is-not-a-standard, like CSS Anchor Positioning under webpositioning). The standard-worthy surface is the semantic vocabulary + container-query wiring + the ResizeObserver fallback contract — not the flex CSS itself.

containerScoped.description: Children reflow against the container box, never the viewport — a FlexRow nested anywhere adapts to its own column, not the window.
containerScoped.rationale: This is the whole point of scope:container (ratified in #467): content-flow altitude responsiveness that composes regardless of where the component is placed, unlike viewport media queries.

nativeFirstFlow.description: Default flow/wrap is pure CSS — `display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap` inside a `container-type:inline-size` context with @container thresholds. No script runs when the platform supports it.
nativeFirstFlow.rationale: Native-first default aligns to the web platform; the block adds vocabulary and a fallback contract, not a JS layout engine. A consumer cannot be conformant to bare `flex-wrap`, so the standard-worthy part is the semantic naming + wiring, not the CSS.

resizeObserverFallback.description: Where @container is unavailable, a ResizeObserver on the container drives the same step thresholds, emitting the identical reflow contract so authored markup is unchanged across both paths.
resizeObserverFallback.rationale: ResizeObserver is the implementation substrate (impl-is-not-a-standard), mirroring how CSS Anchor Positioning sits under webpositioning. The fallback is a graceful-degradation contract, not a separate protocol.

containerQueries.usage: `container-type: inline-size` + `@container` size queries provide the native, script-free reflow path; the FlexRow steps map onto container-query thresholds.

flexWrap.usage: `flex-wrap: wrap` provides the intrinsic auto-flow of children into as many rows as the available inline space allows.

resizeObserver.usage: The non-container-query fallback substrate — observes the container box and re-evaluates the step thresholds on resize, emitting the same reflow contract.
